classical fields

Table of contents

Prerequisites

Functionals

Definition. Let $M$ be a subset of $\bbr^m$, $V$ a real vector space, and write $\calf$ for a subset of the set of all functions $M \to V$. Given an integer $n\geq 1$, a real-valued $n$-functional is a function on the $n$-fold cartesian product $\calf^n$:

\begin{equation}\notag F: \calf^n \to \bbr, \quad \phi \mapsto F(\phi), \end{equation}

where $\phi = (\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_n)$. One obtains complex-valued $n$-functionals by changing $\bbr$ to $\bbc$. Functions in $\calf$ are called $V$-valued fields on $M$.

Blah!

Definition. The variation of an $n$-functional $F$ at $\phi$ in the direction of $\eta$ is the derivative

\begin{equation}\notag \delta F_\phi(\eta) = \frac{\d }{\d \dev} \Bigg|_{\dev=0} F(\phi + \dev \eta). \end{equation}

The functional derivative of $F$ at $\phi$ is a function

\begin{equation}\notag D F_\phi: M \to \Hom_\bbr{(\bbr^n,\bbr)}, \quad x \mapsto DF_\phi(x), \end{equation}

such that

\begin{equation}\notag \delta F_\phi(\eta) = \int \d^m x \ DF_\phi(x) \eta(x). \end{equation}

Note that

\begin{equation}\notag \delta F_\phi(\eta) = \sum_{a=1}^n \frac{\d}{\d \dev}\Bigg|_{\dev=0} F(\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_a+\dev \eta_a,\ldots,\phi_n) = \int \d^m x \ \left\{\sum_{a=1}^n \frac{\delta F(\phi)}{\delta \phi_a(x)} \eta_a(x) \right\} \end{equation}

where the $a$-th partial functional derivative in the integral is obtained by (functionally) differentiating the functional $F$ in the $a$-th slot while holding the other arguments constant:

\begin{equation}\notag \frac{\d}{\d \dev}\Bigg|_{\dev=0} F(\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_a+\dev \eta_a,\ldots,\phi_n) = \int \d^m x \ \frac{\delta F(\phi)}{\delta \phi_a(x)}\eta_a(x). \end{equation}

This suggests that the functional derivative $DF_\phi(x)$ is represented by the row vector of partial functional derivatives:

\begin{equation}\notag [DF_\phi(x)] = \begin{bmatrix} \displaystyle\frac{\delta F(\phi)}{\delta \phi_1(x)} & \cdots & \displaystyle \frac{\delta F(\phi)}{\delta \phi_n(x)} \end{bmatrix}. \end{equation}
